headline: A US government export-control order shutting down Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the month alongside a wave of 1,000-plus tokens-per-second inference breakthroughs.
body: The US government issued an export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers three days after launch, citing jailbreak vulnerability and triggering coverage of frontier-AI access control and geopolitical restriction across multiple sources [3][5][7][8]. In parallel, inference speed broke out: Google's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion techniques to text to exceed 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting local models from memory-bound to compute-bound, while Xiaomi's MiMo-V2.5-Pro-UltraSpeed hit similar throughput via FP4 quantization, speculative decoding, and persistent GPU kernel runtime [1][6][9]. On infrastructure, system architecture, memory systems, KV cache quantization, and vector database all registered breakouts, with Model Context Protocol and JSON-RPC entering as new agentic-tooling entrants. Competitive positioning featured too, with claims that OpenAI's dominance is eroding as Microsoft, Apple, and Google reduce dependence through their own LLM integrations [4].
